reviews.review_description: Aside from the incredibly beautiful design and sets, the fluid shifts in  scale are quite startling. One minute, you're overlooking a scene with grandmother... and the next minute, you're inside the scene. The transitions are seamless and powerful. That said, at end of day, it's the storytelling that really grabs you.

The precise optimization, mechanics, best practice of hand tracking and an unfolding story of a generational cycle from a mother to daughter, a grand daughter to Nana with such a dynamic environment!
This is truly a love letter for those who miss their grandmother. 
Many thanks to writer,director Isabelle Andreani for creating this one of a kind,high-impact experience, and many thanks to Small Creative Studio and SkillLab for bringing up Nana Lou.

At some point we'll all end up experiencing what the characters are going through here. It's a beautiful and touching story, one that draws you in a way that only VR can. You glide from scene to scene, shifting through perspectives, scales, time and points of view. It's a miniature world in which every character, every musical note and every sound tune up to tell a universal story.
